summary: Joe Reis argues that in-person communities are becoming increasingly vital as antidotes to AI-generated content, toxic social media, and widespread loneliness. Writing from Data Day Texas, he emphasizes that as AI permeates every aspect of life, people will seek authentic human connections over algorithmically generated relationships.
key_points: In-person communities serve as antidotes to AI slop, toxic social media, and loneliness
People will increasingly gravitate toward strong communities as they tire of algorithmic relationships
Vendor-free, informal gatherings like Data Day Texas demonstrate the power of genuine connection
The Practical Data Discord community is now open to the public as a practitioner-focused space
money_quotes: In an age of endless AI slop, toxic social media, internetenshitification, and an epidemic of loneliness, communities (especially in person) are an antidote.
I predict people will gravitate to strong communities more than ever as people tire of algorithmically generated relationships.
Spending time with my friends at Data Day Texas reminds me of the power of just…hanging out.
key_insight: As AI increasingly mediates human interaction and content creation, authentic in-person communities will become more valuable than ever as people seek genuine human connection.
